title: Add visualViewport-based keyboard inset to the terminal reply-sheet
action: Fix the OS-keyboard overlap bug in the reply-sheet per CONTEXT.md D1-D4. Add a pure, exported helper computeKeyboardInset(innerHeight, viewportHeight, offsetTop): number that returns Math.max(0, innerHeight - viewportHeight - offsetTop) -- the gap between the layout viewport and window.visualViewport, nonzero exactly when an OS keyboard is showing and the browser did not already shrink 100dvh to compensate (L1 discovery: #app's 100dvh, styles.css:102, does not reliably shrink with the keyboard across browsers -- e.g. Chrome's default interactive-widget=resizes-visual leaves the layout viewport, and thus 100dvh, untouched while only visualViewport shrinks; this is the actual 'mot so browser' split named in the bug report). In openReply (terminal.ts:219-224), after the existing applySheetInset(replySheet) call, register a window.visualViewport 'resize' listener (feature-detected -- per D2, no-op when window.visualViewport is undefined) that recomputes the keyboard inset and: (a) sets .reply-sheet's inline bottom style to the inset in px (satisfies D3 -- keeps the reply-sheet itself, including the Send button, above the keyboard even when 100dvh did not shrink), and (b) adds the same inset on top of the existing overlap-based #term-viewport paddingBottom (keeps terminal content visible above the now-higher sheet). The listener must NEVER call viewport.scrollTop = viewport.scrollHeight (per D4 -- only the initial openReply scroll-to-bottom stays; resize-triggered updates only touch padding/position). Remove the listener in closeReply (terminal.ts:268-272) and reset .reply-sheet's inline bottom back to unset alongside the existing clearSheetInset(). .keys-pad is unchanged -- no keyboard ever opens for it (per D1).

truths: computeKeyboardInset returns 0 when viewportHeight + offsetTop >= innerHeight (no keyboard, or window.visualViewport unsupported)
computeKeyboardInset returns the positive gap (innerHeight - viewportHeight - offsetTop) when the visual viewport is shrunk by the keyboard
openReply registers a visualViewport resize listener only when window.visualViewport exists; closeReply removes it
The resize listener updates #term-viewport paddingBottom and .reply-sheet's bottom offset but never re-forces viewport.scrollTop

artifacts.substantive: exports computeKeyboardInset; openReply/closeReply wire and tear down the visualViewport resize listener
unit tests for computeKeyboardInset covering no-keyboard, keyboard-open, and unsupported-API cases

key_links: openReply's initial inset call and the resize listener share the same computeKeyboardInset/apply logic, so first-open and later resize stay consistent
prohibitions: No change to the existing scroll-to-bottom call inside openReply's initial applySheetInset invocation

trace.outcome: Added pure exported computeKeyboardInset(innerHeight, viewportHeight, offsetTop) helper; openReply now registers a feature-detected window.visualViewport resize listener (reply-sheet only, D1) that sets .reply-sheet's inline bottom and adds the keyboard inset on top of the existing overlap-based #term-viewport paddingBottom, never re-forcing scrollTop (D4); closeReply tears the listener down and resets .reply-sheet's bottom. Graceful no-op when window.visualViewport is undefined (D2).
